than the second herd.] [To find the size of the third herd, we need to subtract 5 from the number of elephants in the second herd.] [24 minus 5 equals 19.] [So, the third herd has 19 elephants.] [Now, has 24 elephants.] [The third herd has 19 elephants.] [Adding these numbers together gives us the total.] [12 plus 24 equals 36.] [36 plus 19 equals 55.] [Therefore, the total number of elephants at the waterhole is 55.] [This calculation is important for understanding the population dynamics of the elephants in the reserve.] [It also helps in monitoring the health and behavior of the herds.] [The indigenous community member uses this information to make informed decisions about conservation efforts.] [Tracking elephant herds is essential for maintaining the balance of the ecosystem.] [Elephants play a crucial role in shaping their environment.] [They help in seed dispersal, soil aeration, and maintaining the structure of the habitat.] [By observing the herds, the community member
